items.text:
Subject: Apartment Maintenance – Please Read

Hi Mike,

I just heard from the building manager about the water pipe inspection scheduled for our apartment. The inspection will take place this Friday, March 15, between 9:00 AM and 12:00 PM. We both need to be present so the plumber can access all rooms. If neither of us can make it, we must reschedule by calling the office before Thursday at 5:00 PM.

Also, the manager reminded me about the $35 late fee for last month's rent. I already paid my half, but I noticed you haven't submitted yours yet. Could you please transfer your share by tomorrow? Let's avoid another penalty.

Best regards,
Jordan

questions.stem: According to the email, what must Jordan and Mike do for the water pipe inspection?

options.A: Call the building manager before Thursday
options.B: Pay a $35 late fee before the inspection
options.C: Be present in the apartment during the inspection
options.D: Reschedule the inspection to next Friday

questions.explanation: The email states 'We both need to be present so the plumber can access all rooms.'

questions.stem: What does Jordan ask Mike to do by tomorrow?

options.B: Transfer his share of the rent

questions.explanation: The email says 'Could you please transfer your share by tomorrow?' referring to Mike's half of the rent.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the $35 late fee mentioned in the email?

options.A: It was charged because the rent was not paid on time
options.B: It is a fee for rescheduling the inspection
options.C: It has already been paid by Jordan
options.D: It must be split equally between both roommates

questions.explanation: The email mentions a '$35 late fee for last month's rent,' implying it resulted from a late payment.

items.text:
Notice: Updated Allergen Policy for Campus Dining Halls

Effective September 1, all on-campus dining halls will implement a revised allergen management policy to enhance student safety. This policy applies to Anderson Hall, Smith Commons, and the Student Center Cafeteria during regular operating hours (7:30 AM to 8:00 PM).

- All menu items will display clear allergen labels for the top nine allergens, including peanuts, tree nuts, dairy, eggs, soy, wheat, fish, shellfish, and sesame.
- Students with severe allergies must register with the Dining Services office by August 25 to receive a personalized meal plan. A $10 processing fee applies.
- Cross-contact prevention measures include separate preparation areas and color-coded utensils.
- Any student who experiences an allergic reaction should report it immediately to the nearest dining hall manager.

For questions, contact Dining Services at (555) 123-4567 or visit Room 204 in the Student Center.

format_metadata.subject: Updated Allergen Policy for Campus Dining Halls

questions.stem: According to the notice, what must students with severe allergies do by August 25?

options.A: Report any allergic reactions to a manager
options.B: Review the updated allergen labels on menus
options.D: Register with the Dining Services office

questions.explanation: The notice states 'Students with severe allergies must register with the Dining Services office by August 25 to receive a personalized meal plan.'

questions.stem: The word 'measures' in the notice is closest in meaning to
questions.explanation: In the context of 'Cross-contact prevention measures,' 'measures' refers to actions or steps taken to prevent allergen cross-contact.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the dining halls before September 1?

options.A: They did not require allergen labels on all menu items
options.B: They charged a higher processing fee for meal plans
options.C: They only operated during breakfast and lunch hours
options.D: They did not allow students with severe allergies to eat there

questions.explanation: The notice says the new policy is 'Effective September 1,' implying that before that date, the allergen labeling requirement was not in place.
